description:
Offered: every third semester
This course explores a wide range of fiction and literary practices of some leading African writers. The course pays close attention to particular oral, literary and visual strategies used by African writers to create multiple versions of “life” in Africa during pre-colonial, colonial and contemporary times. It also raises and engages a wide range of crucial questions and ideas that pertain to African cultures, politics and societies.
Prerequisite(s): ENGL 1200
Meets General Education Requirement(s): AIA, DP, LI
